title: Oscars 2026: Jessie Buckley Wins Best Actress Oscar
text: Irish actress Jessie Buckley from Killarney won the Academy Award for Best Actress at the 2026 Oscars ceremony in Los Angeles for her role as Agnes Shakespeare in Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet. She became the first Irish woman to win this award. Buckley wore a striking Chanel dress with a dramatic horizontal red sash. After winning multiple major awards throughout the season including the Golden Globes, BAFTA, and Actor Awards, she arrived as an overwhelming favorite but appeared genuinely surprised by the win. In her acceptance speech, Buckley thanked her fellow nominees and expressed gratitude to her parents for encouraging them to dream and avoid being limited by expectations. She addressed her husband Freddie Sorensen, expressing her love and calling him the most incredible dad. She concluded by dedicating the award to the beautiful chaos of a mother's heart, noting it was Mother's Day in the UK. Richard Baneham from Dublin also won Best Visual Effects for Avatar: Fire and Ash, becoming only the second Irish citizen to earn three Oscars.
